$comment: Cohort manifest for the public-AUT cold-start cohort. Authoritative for which AUTs sit on which side of the clean-room partition (see docs/v2-cold-start-cohort-spike.md §4.4). The trust-policy gate must consult this manifest at runtime to refuse canon graduation in held-out contexts (C2). Promotion held-out → training is allowed and irreversible (C4); training → held-out is forbidden.

auts.name: todomvc
auts.notes: React variant. Has notable real-world ARIA imperfections (anonymous destroy buttons with no accessible name, per-todo <label> with no for attribute, no aria-current on selected filter, no explicit landmark roles). Surface inventory captured in docs/v2-cold-start-todomvc-journal.md Entry 3.

auts.name: httpbin-form
auts.promotionReason: Cycles 5-7 used httpbin failures (Probe Seeds 10, 11) to drive classifier code changes. The held-out partition is contaminated; promoting to training per spike §4.4 C4 (one-way promotion held-out -> training is allowed and irreversible). See journal Entry 32.
auts.notes: Pizza-order form demo at httpbin.org. Different shape from TodoMVC training (server-rendered HTML, wrapping <label> elements with no for attribute, inputs with name but no id/aria-label). First introduced as held-out in cycle 5; promoted to training in cycle 8 after acknowledging that operator inspection during cycles 6-7 fed observe-side classifier improvements.
